prompt: A catalog client stops after the first page when a service includes normal descriptive punctuation in its HTTP Link metadata. In another deployment, the client sends a relative next-page reference directly to the transport instead of resolving it against the page that supplied it. Simple absolute Link headers continue to paginate, and item decoding is unchanged. Preserve the parser and collection APIs, including relation tokens that share one rel parameter.

repo_files.page_client.py:
from collections.abc import Callable, Mapping

from item_decoder import decode_items
from link_header import link_for_relation


Response = tuple[str, Mapping[str, str]]
Fetch = Callable[[str], Response]


def collect_items(start_url: str, fetch: Fetch) -> tuple[dict[str, object], ...]:
    collected = []
    current_url: str | None = start_url
    while current_url is not None:
        body, headers = fetch(current_url)
        collected.extend(decode_items(body))
        current_url = link_for_relation(headers.get("Link", ""), "next")
    return tuple(collected)
